Container.Name: keyanu
Container.Overview:
Keyanu — a self-hosted infrastructure credential manager for homelabs and system administrators, built for pfSense, Unraid, MikroTik, Home Assistant, Cloudflare, GitHub, and anything else you manage credentials for.
This single container includes the web UI, the API, and the database engine -- there is nothing else to install and no separate containers or custom Docker networks to configure. All settings below are plain environment variables, editable any time from this container's Edit screen.
On first boot, an admin account is created from ADMIN_USERNAME / ADMIN_PASSWORD. You will be required to set a new password on first login.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port the Keyanu web UI (and API) is served on

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Stores the SQLite database and uploaded files. Back up this folder.

Config.Name: SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: Signs session tokens. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32

Config.Name: ENCRYPTION_KEY
Config.Description: Encrypts credential and TOTP secrets at rest. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. NEVER change this once data exists -- doing so makes stored credentials permanently unreadable. Back it up separately from Unraid's appdata backups.

Config.Name: ADMIN_USERNAME
Config.Description: Username for the initial (single) user account

Config.Name: ADMIN_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Temporary password for the initial admin account -- you will be forced to change it on first login

Config.Name: DATA_DIR
Config.Description: Internal container path for the database and files. Must match the container-side path of the Appdata volume above. Leave as /data unless you changed that too.

Config.Name: DEFAULT_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES
Config.Description: Idle session timeout in minutes applied to the admin account on first boot (720 = 12 hours). Changeable later per-account under Settings > Security.
